steps.title: Read the graph
steps.content: The graph shows $y = f(x) + 11$. Subtracting 11 from the y-values of the line gives the graph of $y = f(x)$. From the graph, $f(x)$ is a line with a negative slope and a negative y-intercept.

steps.title: Match the form
steps.content:
With $c, d \gt 0$: Choice A — $f(x) = -d - cx$ is $f(x) = -d - cx$, which has slope $-c \lt 0$ and y-intercept $-d \lt 0$. That matches both signs.

B has positive slope and intercept; C has positive slope; D has positive intercept.

That matches Choice A — $f(x) = -d - cx$.
